frameworks.name: neuronal circuit / attractor network model
frameworks.one_line: distributed spiking network whose dynamics produce uncertainty signals and change-of-mind

frameworks.name: decision confidence
frameworks.one_line: confidence is read out from circuit dynamics rather than imposed by a separate module

frameworks.name: noise-driven multistable dynamical systems
frameworks.one_line: transient choice-neutral stable steady state plus noise triggers reversal

core_question: Can a single neural circuit jointly compute decision uncertainty and produce change-of-mind, unifying their behavioural and neural signatures?
key_assumptions: decision-making is implemented by recurrent neuronal network dynamics
decision uncertainty is the complement of confidence and can be read from circuit state
change-of-mind arises from internal dynamics rather than new external evidence
manipulations: varied evidence / task conditions known to elicit confidence and change-of-mind
compared sensory-based vs motor-based neuronal populations in the distributed network
key_findings: a neuronal circuit model reproduces a variety of behavioural and neural data on confidence and change-of-mind
change-of-mind emerges from a transient uncertainty-induced choice-neutral stable steady state with noisy fluctuations
the neural signature of change-of-mind is more distinctively identifiable in motor-based neurons
interpretations: decision confidence and change-of-mind can be unified within one circuit framework
motor-stage representations carry the clearest correlate of reversal of choice
